downstream_consequence: PASS licenses only the toy claim below; a fired control would halt it.
fact: With 12 anchors the linear calibration recovers the latent scalar to the reported RMSE; the shuffled-anchor control does not.
inference: The recovery is carried by the calibration signal, not by the raw reading alone.
metric: RMSE(recovered, latent) on held-out samples (lower is better)
question: Does anchor calibration recover a latent scalar from a scaled-and-shifted noisy reading, where a shuffled-anchor control cannot?
what_was_not_shown: This is a toy demonstration of the harness, not a real-world disentanglement result.
